summary: Vega Health supports ASTP/ONC's deregulatory efforts to reduce burdens on health IT innovators, emphasizing the need to address information blocking by incumbents and to prepare for increased FHIR API demands from AI-native companies. They highlight the importance of transparent AI evaluation and urge focus on outcome-based regulation rather than development-based restrictions.
key_arguments: Information blocking by incumbent vendors remains a major barrier to new entrants and innovation.
Regulatory focus should be on harmful AI outcomes rather than on development processes to avoid stifling innovation.
Simplifying and aligning health IT security, interoperability, and privacy requirements will foster AI adoption across health systems of all sizes.
Certified health IT vendors use complex regulations to obstruct lawful data access, disadvantaging competitors and innovation.
recommendations: ASTP/ONC should establish controls to prevent certified health IT vendors from leveraging market power to block or disadvantage competitors.
Focus enforcement on unlawful outcomes rather than imposing preemptive developer-facing regulations.
Support transparent, objective evaluation frameworks for AI solutions in the absence of federally mandated model cards.
Continue efforts to reduce regulatory, administrative, and artificial barriers that hinder new market entrants.
notable_quote: Vega Health applauds ASTP/ONC’s deliberate efforts to reduce the regulatory, administrative, and artificial barriers that have been erected by large incumbents.
